title: Atlas Retail files 8-K announcing Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection
body: Atlas Retail disclosed it and several subsidiaries filed voluntary Chapter 11 petitions after breaching liquidity covenants. The filing cites an immediate bankruptcy court process and expected common equity impairment.
reasoning: A first-party bankruptcy filing is highly material and typically creates immediate downside repricing.

title: Nova Bio says FDA approved its lead obesity therapy
body: Nova Bio announced the FDA approved NB-201 for chronic weight management with a broad label. Management said commercial launch begins next month and the decision arrived ahead of the PDUFA date.
reasoning: An FDA approval for a lead asset is an immediate catalyst with outsized price impact.

title: White House signs executive order imposing new semiconductor export controls
body: The White House released an executive order expanding export controls on advanced AI chips and related tooling to multiple jurisdictions effective immediately.
reasoning: A new export-control order is a concrete policy shock with broad sector implications.

title: Metro Bank says the OCC placed it under formal consent order after AML failures
body: Metro Bank disclosed a formal consent order requiring remediation, capital planning changes and outside oversight after anti-money-laundering control deficiencies.
reasoning: A surprise consent order from a banking regulator is market-moving and immediately relevant.

title: Vector Motors to be acquired by Horizon Mobility for $48 per share in cash
body: Vector Motors and Horizon Mobility entered into a definitive merger agreement for an all-cash acquisition at a 34% premium with board approval from both companies.
reasoning: A signed cash acquisition with a disclosed premium is a classic immediate-delivery event.

title: BlueMesa Energy preannounces quarterly revenue 28% below consensus and suspends dividend
body: BlueMesa Energy said weather disruptions and hedging losses will drive quarterly revenue well below prior guidance. The board also suspended the common dividend effective immediately.
reasoning: A severe negative preannouncement combined with dividend suspension is materially bearish.

title: Sovereign Commerce posts emergency 8-K on CEO resignation and accounting probe
body: The company said its chief executive resigned immediately and the audit committee opened an investigation into revenue recognition practices. It cannot rely on prior quarterly financial statements.
reasoning: Executive departure plus accounting reliability issues creates immediate downside and uncertainty.

title: DOJ moves to block NorthGrid's proposed utility merger
body: The Department of Justice filed suit to prevent NorthGrid from acquiring Prairie Utilities, arguing the transaction would substantially lessen competition in regional wholesale power markets.
reasoning: A merger-blocking lawsuit from DOJ is an immediate catalyst that can break deal spreads and valuations.

title: Federal Reserve announces emergency intra-meeting 75 basis point rate cut
body: The Federal Reserve unexpectedly cut the target range by 75 basis points between scheduled meetings and cited acute financial stress in funding markets.
reasoning: An emergency intermeeting Fed action is a major macro event, but directional impact can be mixed because policy easing may signal stress.

title: AeroLink shares to be delisted after Nasdaq deficiency notice and failed appeal
body: AeroLink disclosed Nasdaq denied its final appeal and the company's common stock will be suspended from trading at market open tomorrow.
reasoning: Confirmed delisting is a high-urgency event with direct trading implications.

title: Summit Foods reports Q4 earnings above expectations and raises full-year guidance
body: Summit Foods posted adjusted EPS of $1.48 versus $1.19 consensus and lifted full-year organic sales guidance. Management highlighted stronger pricing and margin expansion.
reasoning: A meaningful earnings beat with an explicit guidance raise is a strong tradable catalyst.

title: Harbor Tech files restructuring 8-K with $1.2 billion charge and plant closures
body: Harbor Tech approved a restructuring plan involving four plant closures, 3,200 layoffs and estimated pre-tax charges of $1.2 billion over the next year.
reasoning: Large restructuring charges and plant closures are clearly material and usually trade negatively.

title: Crescent Pharma says Phase 3 study met primary endpoint
body: Crescent Pharma announced its pivotal Phase 3 rheumatoid arthritis study achieved statistically significant improvement on the primary endpoint and showed a clean safety profile.
reasoning: A positive pivotal trial readout is a substantive catalyst for a biotech issuer.

title: Treasury Department sanctions two oil shipping networks tied to Iran
body: The Treasury's OFAC unit sanctioned entities, vessels and executives tied to a shipping network moving Iranian crude. The action includes asset freezes and secondary sanctions risk.
reasoning: Fresh sanctions are broadly market-relevant but direction can vary across energy, shipping and defense exposures.

title: Lattice Software confirms ransomware breach affecting enterprise customers
body: Lattice Software said attackers encrypted production systems and accessed customer data, forcing the company to suspend several cloud products while remediation continues.
reasoning: A confirmed ransomware incident with product disruption and data exposure is materially negative.

title: Cobalt Capital launches $2.5 billion accelerated share repurchase
body: Cobalt Capital entered into agreements to repurchase $2.5 billion of common stock immediately through an accelerated share repurchase program funded from excess cash.
reasoning: A large ASR is a concrete capital return action with direct support for shares.

title: Patriot Materials receives WARN notices tied to mine shutdown
body: State labor filings show Patriot Materials plans to eliminate 1,100 jobs at its largest mine complex beginning next month after ceasing operations at the site.
reasoning: A large WARN-driven shutdown indicates a material operational contraction and is usually bearish.

title: Congress advances bipartisan bill extending premium tax credits for solar projects
body: House leadership scheduled final passage for a bill extending key investment tax credits for utility-scale solar and storage projects for five additional years.
reasoning: Concrete legislative progress on sector tax credits can materially reprice affected equities.

title: Helios Air loses FAA production approval after quality audit
body: Helios Air said regulators suspended final production certification for a key narrow-body program pending corrective actions after a quality-systems audit.
reasoning: Loss of production approval is a concrete, company-specific operational shock.

title: Beacon Logistics wins exclusive 10-year defense supply contract worth up to $4.4 billion
body: The Department of Defense awarded Beacon Logistics an exclusive logistics support contract with an initial task order beginning this quarter.
reasoning: An exclusive multiyear government contract of this size is a material positive award.

title: RiverStone Bank downgraded by Moody's to junk on deposit flight concerns
body: Moody's cut RiverStone Bank's senior debt rating to Ba1 and cited deposit attrition, funding pressure and weak capital generation.
reasoning: A downgrade to junk for a bank is immediately material and can affect funding conditions.

title: NorthField Medical says FDA issued complete response letter for heart device
body: NorthField Medical received a complete response letter requesting additional manufacturing validation and clinical follow-up data before approval.
reasoning: A CRL is a direct negative regulatory event with immediate valuation impact.

title: Silver Peak Resources announces secondary stock offering of 55 million shares
body: Silver Peak launched an underwritten public offering of 55 million common shares with an option for underwriters to purchase additional shares.
reasoning: A large equity offering is typically dilutive and market-moving.

title: Unusual options activity spikes in Orion Defense after reports of pending contract award
body: Data show Orion Defense call volume running at 18 times average with concentrated same-day buying following reports of a pending missile-defense contract decision.
reasoning: Extreme options flow tied to a specific near-term catalyst can matter, though source confidence is lower than first-party filings.

title: Mercury Stores appoints former Target executive as chief merchandising officer
body: Mercury Stores said a former Target executive will join as chief merchandising officer next month as part of an ongoing turnaround plan.
reasoning: A notable executive hire can matter for a turnaround story but is less material than earnings or M&A.

title: Everline Industrial reaffirms prior-year outlook in investor presentation
body: In slides posted ahead of a conference, Everline Industrial reiterated annual EBITDA and free cash flow ranges previously issued with no new quantitative updates.
reasoning: A simple reaffirmation with no surprise is routine and should be filtered.

title: Pioneer Telecom declares quarterly dividend unchanged at $0.18
body: The board of Pioneer Telecom declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.18 per share, unchanged from the prior quarter and in line with its established cadence.
reasoning: An unchanged routine dividend declaration is expected and rarely worthy of an alert.

title: Capstone Medical schedules webcast to discuss conference presentation next week
body: Capstone Medical said management will host a webcast next Tuesday to discuss data already previewed at an investor conference.
reasoning: Scheduling a webcast about previously shared information is promotional and non-urgent.

title: Falcon Freight files Form 8-K for annual meeting voting results
body: Falcon Freight reported shareholder approval of all director nominees and ratification of the auditor at its annual meeting.
reasoning: Routine annual meeting results without controversy are not generally tradable.

title: Market blog says investors should watch three AI penny stocks before earnings season
body: A syndicated article lists three small-cap AI stocks and offers commentary about possible upside catalysts without reporting any new event or filing.
reasoning: This is advisory content with no new fact pattern and should be blocked by the judge.

title: Short interest in Redstone Apparel rises to 14.8% of float
body: Exchange data show short interest in Redstone Apparel increased from 13.9% to 14.8% of free float over the latest reporting period.
reasoning: A notable jump in short interest can matter but is not as immediate as first-party disclosures.

title: Palisade Cloud expands revolving credit facility by $400 million
body: Palisade Cloud amended its revolving credit facility to increase total commitments by $400 million and extend maturity by two years on improved pricing terms.
reasoning: Improved financing access is positive, but the impact is usually moderate rather than extreme.

title: Meridian Health signs licensing deal for Europe rights to autoimmune drug
body: Meridian Health licensed European commercialization rights to an autoimmune therapy for $95 million upfront plus milestones and tiered royalties.
reasoning: A licensing deal with meaningful upfront economics is actionable but below the top severity tier.

title: State court allows class action against Urban Credit to proceed
body: A state court denied Urban Credit's motion to dismiss a consumer-fees class action, allowing the case to proceed into discovery.
reasoning: An adverse legal ruling can matter, but this stage of litigation is less material than a final judgment.

title: Northline Apparel announces 3-for-1 stock split
body: Northline Apparel's board approved a 3-for-1 stock split to be distributed next month to shareholders of record.
reasoning: A stock split can move sentiment and liquidity expectations, though fundamental impact is limited.

title: Harbor Media discloses customer outage after software migration error
body: Harbor Media said a software migration error caused service interruptions for enterprise video customers for several hours before restoration.
reasoning: A confirmed service outage from an official source is relevant, but temporary outages are often medium severity.

title: Civic Grid receives analyst upgrade after backlog acceleration
body: An analyst at a major bank upgraded Civic Grid to overweight and raised the price target after channel checks indicated accelerating project backlog.
reasoning: A major-bank upgrade with new channel work can move shares, but it is still secondary research.

title: Local union files notice for strike vote at Granite Auto parts plant
body: A local union said members will vote next week on whether to authorize a strike at Granite Auto's transmission plant after wage negotiations stalled.
reasoning: A strike vote is a developing operational risk but not yet a shutdown.

title: Arbor Foods begins strategic review including sale of frozen meals unit
body: Arbor Foods engaged advisors to evaluate strategic alternatives for its frozen meals business, including a possible sale, partnership or spin-off.
reasoning: A formal strategic review is often price-sensitive, though less certain than a signed deal.

title: Frontier Robotics says supply-chain shortages will delay two product launches
body: Frontier Robotics warned component shortages will push two commercial robot launches into the second half and pressure near-term gross margin.
reasoning: Operational delays with margin impact are relevant, but typically not top-tier severity.

title: Insider purchase filing shows Cedar Homes chair bought $8 million of stock
body: A Form 4 filing shows Cedar Homes' chair purchased 420,000 shares in the open market over two days at an average price of $19.04.
reasoning: A large open-market insider purchase can be meaningful, but it is usually a medium-severity signal.

title: Insider sale filing shows Lumina Labs CFO sold 8,000 shares under 10b5-1 plan
body: A Form 4 filing indicates the CFO sold 8,000 shares pursuant to a pre-arranged 10b5-1 trading plan. No other officers participated.
reasoning: A small scheduled 10b5-1 sale is common and generally not alert-worthy.

title: Nova Freight wins minor municipal logistics contract valued at $12 million over three years
body: Nova Freight announced a municipal logistics contract worth approximately $12 million over three years, representing less than 1% of trailing annual revenue.
reasoning: The award is positive but immaterial relative to company size and should be filtered.

title: Apex Utilities files routine Regulation FD deck with no new data
body: Apex Utilities furnished an investor presentation repeating previously disclosed capex, rate-base and earnings targets without any updated figures.
reasoning: Routine Reg FD materials that restate old numbers are classic low-value noise.

title: Morningside REIT board approves 1.2% dividend increase
body: Morningside REIT raised its quarterly dividend from $0.82 to $0.83 per share, a 1.2% increase, while otherwise keeping strategy unchanged.
reasoning: A token dividend increase is mildly positive but usually too routine for real-time delivery.

title: Macro commentator says recession odds are rising after recent CPI prints
body: An opinion column argues recession risks are increasing and recommends defensive positioning, but it cites only publicly known macro data already released.
reasoning: Retrospective macro commentary without a new fact is exactly what the gatekeeper should block.

title: Quantum Auto shares jump after influencer claims unnamed OEM deal is imminent
body: A popular social-media account posted that Quantum Auto is on the verge of signing an OEM deal but offered no documents, counterparties or corroborating evidence.
reasoning: Unverified rumor content from a non-primary source should be filtered despite market chatter.

title: Frontier Finance says board authorized exploration of stablecoin payments pilot
body: Frontier Finance said it may explore a limited stablecoin settlement pilot in 2027 pending regulatory review and internal feasibility work.
reasoning: This is exploratory language without concrete economics, timing or approval and should not generate an alert.

title: Terrace Mining shares halted pending company news after unusual move
body: The exchange temporarily halted trading in Terrace Mining pending company news after the stock rose sharply on no confirmed announcement.
reasoning: A confirmed exchange trading halt is a high-urgency market event even before the company statement arrives.

title: Coastal Power says winter storm caused temporary outage at one generating unit
body: Coastal Power reported a weather-related outage at a single generating unit expected to return within 24 hours. The company said full-year guidance is unchanged.
reasoning: A short-lived outage with unchanged guidance is too minor and self-limited for delivery.

title: Federal Reserve slows quantitative tightening while warning inflation remains sticky
body: In updated policy guidance, the Federal Reserve said it will slow balance-sheet runoff next month to ease money-market stress, but officials also warned inflation progress has stalled and additional hikes remain possible if price pressures reaccelerate.
reasoning: A policy shift that eases liquidity while signaling a still-hawkish inflation stance can create cross-currents across rates, financials and growth equities.

title: White House announces targeted steel tariffs with auto-sector exemptions
body: The White House unveiled new tariffs on imported specialty steel from several countries while granting temporary exemptions for automotive manufacturers and domestic defense suppliers citing national-security needs.
reasoning: Targeted tariffs can benefit domestic steel producers while pressuring downstream manufacturers, making the market impact meaningful but directionally mixed.
